title: eriogonum vimineum subsp. gracile
intro: Eriogonum vimineum subsp. gracile, also known as the slender redstem filaree or wire-lettuce, is a subspecies of the Eriogonum vimineum plant, native to North America. This shrub-like plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum vimineum subsp. gracile include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant often include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
